ru.name: Git и GitHub: Твоё первое портфолио
ru.short_description: От нуля до первого проекта на GitHub - практический путь джуна
ru.description:
## 🚀 Зачем этот курс?

Ты приходишь на собеседование. Рекрутер спрашивает: *"Покажите ваш GitHub"*. А у тебя... пусто. Знакомо?

**93% работодателей** смотрят GitHub перед приглашением на интервью. Без активного профиля твои шансы получить оффер падают в 3 раза.

## 💡 Что ты получишь?

**Реальное портфолио** которое работает:
- 🌐 Живой сайт в интернете (GitHub Pages)
- 🔥 История коммитов показывающая что ты умеешь работать с Git
- ✅ Автоматические тесты и CI/CD
- 🎯 Pull Request'ы и Code Review в портфолио
- 📊 GitHub профиль который впечатляет

## 🎬 История успеха

**Алекс, 23 года** - прошёл курс за выходные. Через 2 недели получил первое собеседование. Показал свой GitHub - работодатель увидел что Алекс:
- Умеет работать с Git (50+ коммитов)
- Создаёт осмысленные Pull Request'ы
- Пишет README и документацию
- Настраивает CI/CD

**Результат:** Оффер junior frontend за $1200/мес через месяц! 🎉

## 📚 Программа

5 практических уроков от простого к сложному:

1️⃣ **Основы Git** - Первый репозиторий, коммиты, деплой
2️⃣ **Ветки и merge** - Работа с feature-ветками, конфликты
3️⃣ **Командная работа** - Fork, Pull Requests, Code Review
4️⃣ **Автоматизация** - GitHub Actions, CI/CD, автотесты
5️⃣ **Pro-уровень** - Rebase, cherry-pick, hooks, bisect

## 🎯 Для кого?

✅ Начинающие разработчики без опыта с Git
✅ Студенты готовящиеся к первой работе
✅ Самоучки которым нужно портфолио
✅ Те кто хочет понимать Git на практике

**Никаких скучных лекций** - только практика! Каждый шаг = реальное действие в твоём проекте.

👉 **Начни прямо сейчас** и через пару дней у тебя будет что показать работодателю!

lessons.name: День 1: Твой первый проект на GitHub
lessons.short_description: 📱 История: Алекс решил стать разработчиком. Первый шаг - создать своё портфолио

steps.name: Знакомство с героем
steps.description:
👋 Привет! Меня зовут Алекс и я хочу стать разработчиком.

Сегодня я иду на собеседование. Рекрутер спросила: 'Покажи свой GitHub профиль'. А у меня его нет! 😰

Я понял что нужно создать портфолио. Давай вместе пройдём этот путь!

**🎯 Цель урока:** Создать свой первый проект на GitHub

**📚 Что узнаешь:**
- Что такое Git и зачем он нужен
- Как создать аккаунт на GitHub
- Как опубликовать свой первый сайт в интернете

**⏱️ Время:** 30-40 минут

**🔗 Полезные ссылки:**
- [Что такое Git простыми словами](https://git-scm.com/book/ru/v2)
- [GitHub для начинающих (видео 10 мин)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RGOj5yH7evk)
steps.self_check: Ты понял зачем нужен GitHub и готов начать
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Если что-то не получается:**

- **Перезапусти терминал**
  - Закрой и открой заново
  - Некоторые изменения применяются только после перезапуска
  
- **Очисти кэш браузера**
  - Нажми Ctrl+Shift+R (Windows/Linux)
  - Cmd+Shift+R (Mac)
  
- **Проверь что все команды выполнены по порядку**
  - Вернись на шаг назад
  - Выполни всё заново внимательно

steps.name: Установка Git
steps.description:
💼 Создадим твою профессиональную витрину!

**Что такое GitHub:**
Представ социальную сеть для программистов. Здесь:
- Хранятся проекты
- Работодатели смотрят твои работы
- Можно показать что ты умеешь

**💡 Совет:** Выбери username который не стыдно показать работодателю
- ✅ Хорошо: alex-petrov, maria_dev, ivan-codes
- ❌ Плохо: killer228, mega_hacker, pup-sik

**🔗 Примеры хороших профилей:**
- https://github.com/torvalds (создатель Linux)
- https://github.com/gaearon (создатель React)
steps.actions:
**Шаги регистрации:**

1. 🌐 Открой https://github.com

2. 📝 Нажми большую зелёную кнопку **Sign up**

3. ✉️ Введи свой email (настоящий, туда придёт письмо)

4. 👤 Придумай username (это будет твоя ссылка)

5. 🔐 Создай пароль (минимум 8 символов)

6. 🤖 Пройди проверку 'Я не робот'

7. ✅ Нажми **Create account**

8. 📧 Проверь почту и кликни на ссылку подтверждения

9. 📸 **Важно:** Настрой профиль:
   - Settings → Profile
   - Загрузи фото (или аватар)
   - Заполни Name (настоящее имя)
   - Напиши Bio: 'Начинающий разработчик 💻'

**⏱️ GitHub даст тебе опрос - можешь пропустить (Skip)**
self_check_items: Зайди на github.com/твой-username - видишь свою страницу
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Проблемы при регистрации:**

- **Письмо не пришло?**
  - Проверь папку "Спам" в почте
  - Попробуй другой email (Gmail, Mail.ru)
  - Подожди 5 минут - иногда письма идут долго

- **Username занят?**
  - Добавь цифры: `alex-dev-2024`
  - Добавь свою специализацию: `alex-frontend`
  - Используй дефис вместо подчёркивания

- **Пароль не принимает?**
  - Минимум 8 символов
  - Должны быть буквы И цифры
  - Пример: `MyPass2024`

steps.name: Настройка Git
steps.description:
🎨 Скажем Git кто ты.

**Зачем:** Git будет подписывать твои изменения твоим именем. Так на GitHub будет видно кто автор кода.
steps.actions:
**Открой терминал и выполни 2 команды:**

```bash
# Замени 'Алекс Петров' на своё имя
git config --global user.name "Алекс Петров"

# Замени на свой email с GitHub
git config --global user.email "alex@example.com"
```

**❗ Важно:** Email должен быть ТОЧНО как на GitHub!

**Проверка:**
```bash
# Посмотри что сохранилось
git config user.name
git config user.email
```

Должно показать твои данные.
self_check_items: `git config user.email` показывает email с GitHub
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Команды не работают:**

- **"git: command not found"**
  - Скачай Git: https://git-scm.com/downloads
  - После установки перезапусти терминал
  - Проверь: `git --version`

- **Кавычки не те**
  - Используй прямые кавычки: `"текст"`
  - НЕ используй « » или " "
  
- **Имя с пробелами**
  - Оберни в кавычки: `"Алекс Петров"`

steps.name: Создаём репозиторий
steps.description:
📦 Создадим коробку для твоего проекта!

**Простыми словами:**
Репозиторий = папка для проекта на GitHub

**Зачем:**
- Там будет храниться твой код
- Можно показать работодателю
- Будет ссылка: github.com/твой-username/my-portfolio

**🎯 Мы создадим проект 'my-portfolio' - твоё будущее портфолио!**

**🔗 Что почитать:**
- [Что такое репозиторий](https://docs.github.com/ru/repositories)
steps.actions:
**Пошаговая инструкция:**

1. 🏠 Зайди на https://github.com (должен быть залогинен)

2. ➕ Найди зелёную кнопку **New** (слева вверху)
   - Или открой: https://github.com/new

3. 📝 Заполни форму:
   
   **Repository name:** `my-portfolio`
   
   **Description:** `Мой первый проект - сайт-визитка 🌟`
   
   **Выбери:** 🌍 **Public** (чтобы все видели)
   
   **✅ Поставь галочку:** Add a README file
   
4. 🎉 Нажми зелёную кнопку **Create repository**

5. 🎊 Поздравляю! Ты создал свой первый репозиторий!

**Что ты видишь:**
- Файл README.md (описание проекта)
- Адрес: github.com/твой-username/my-portfolio
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Не получается создать репозиторий:**

- **Имя занято?**
  - Добавь цифру: `my-portfolio-2024`
  - Используй дефис: `my-first-portfolio`
  
- **Кнопка New не активна?**
  - Проверь что ты залогинен (иконка профиля справа вверху)
  - Попробуй: https://github.com/new
  
- **Не можешь подтвердить email?**
  - Settings → Emails → Resend verification

steps.name: Скачиваем проект на компьютер
steps.description:
💾 Скопируем проект с GitHub к себе.

**Простыми словами:**
Клонирование = скачивание проекта с GitHub на твой компьютер

**Зачем:**
- Чтобы редактировать код
- Чтобы добавлять файлы
- Потом отправим обратно на GitHub

**🔗 Видео инструкция:** [Как клонировать репозиторий (2 мин)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CKcqniGu3tA)
steps.actions:
**Шаг 1: Копируем ссылку**

1. Открой свой репозиторий на GitHub
2. Нажми зелёную кнопку **Code**
3. Выбери вкладку **HTTPS**
4. Нажми 📋 (скопировать)
5. Ссылка выглядит так: `https://github.com/username/my-portfolio.git`

**Шаг 2: Клонируем в терминале**

```bash
# Создай папку для проектов
mkdir ~/projects
cd ~/projects

# Вставь свою ссылку вместо USERNAME
git clone https://github.com/USERNAME/my-portfolio.git

# Перейди в проект
cd my-portfolio

# Посмотри что внутри
ls
```

**Что ты увидишь:**
```
README.md
```

✅ Отлично! Проект скачан!
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Проблемы с клонированием:**

- **"Permission denied"**
  - Проверь что репозиторий Public
  - Используй HTTPS ссылку (не SSH)
  
- **Папка не создаётся**
  - Проверь что ты в нужной папке: `pwd`
  - Создай папку вручную: `mkdir ~/projects`
  
- **"Already exists"**
  - Удали старую папку: `rm -rf my-portfolio`
  - Или используй другое имя

steps.name: Создаём HTML страницу
steps.description:
🎨 Начнём с простого HTML!

**Что создадим:**
- Базовую структуру HTML
- Заголовок с твоим именем
- Короткое описание

**💡 Не переживай** - код простой, просто скопируй!
steps.actions:
**Открой редактор:** 🔹 VS Code (рекомендую) - https://code.visualstudio.com 🔹 Sublime Text 🔹 Блокнот **Создай файл index.html:** 1. Открой папку my-portfolio 2. Создай файл: index.html 3. Вставь код: ```html Моя визитка

👋 Привет! Я Алекс

Начинающий разработчик

``` 4. Замени 'Алекс' на своё имя 5. Сохрани (Ctrl+S) 6. Открой в браузере (двойной клик)
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Проблемы с HTML:**

- **Страница не открывается**
  - Проверь имя файла: точно `index.html`?
  - Файл в правильной папке?
  - Попробуй открыть через браузер: File → Open
  
- **Ничего не видно**
  - Проверь что файл сохранён (Ctrl+S)
  - Обнови страницу (Ctrl+R или F5)
  
- **Кривая кодировка (кракозябры)**
  - Проверь `<meta charset="UTF-8">` в коде

steps.name: Первый коммит
steps.description:
💾 Сохраним базовую HTML структуру!

**Важно:** Делаем коммит после каждого логического изменения.
steps.actions:
```bash
cd ~/projects/my-portfolio

# Посмотри что изменилось
git status

# Добавь файл
git add index.html

# Сделай коммит
git commit -m "Создал базовую HTML структуру"

# Посмотри историю
git log --oneline
```
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Проблемы с коммитом:**

- **"nothing to commit"**
  - Сначала добавь файлы: `git add .`
  - Проверь статус: `git status`
  
- **"Please tell me who you are"**
  - Настрой имя: `git config --global user.name "Твоё имя"`
  - Настрой email: `git config --global user.email "email@example.com"`
  
- **Открылся VIM редактор?**
  - Нажми `Esc`
  - Набери `:q!`
  - Нажми Enter

steps.description:

- Будет видна вся история коммитов
- Другие смогут посмотреть твой код
steps.actions:
```bash
git push origin main
```

⏳ **Git отправляет...**

**Если попросит авторизацию:**

🔐 Username: твой-github-username
🔑 Password: НЕ пароль! Нужен токен!

---

### 🎫 Создание токена (если нужно):

1. https://github.com/settings/tokens
2. **Generate new token** → **Classic**
3. Note: `My computer`
4. Expiration: 90 days
5. Scopes: ✅ **repo** (все галочки)
6. **Generate token**
7. 📋 **СКОПИРУЙ** и сохрани!
8. Вставь вместо пароля

---

✅ **Успех:**
```
   abc1234..xyz5678  main -> main
```

steps.name: История второго дня
steps.description:
📖 Продолжаем историю Алекса...

**Что произошло:**
Алекс показал свой сайт другу-программисту. Друг сказал: 'Круто! А добавь секцию с проектами!'

Алекс начал добавлять, но испугался: 'А вдруг что-то сломаю? Сайт же уже работает!'

Друг объяснил про **ветки** (branches):

🌳 **Ветка = копия кода для экспериментов**

**Как это работает:**
- main = стабильная версия (сайт работает)
- feature-ветка = экспериментальная (можно ломать)
- Если эксперимент удался → сливаем в main
- Если нет → просто удаляем ветку

**Аналогия:**
Представь что у тебя есть сочинение (main). Ты хочешь попробовать переписать введение, но не уверен что получится лучше. Создаёшь копию документа (ветку), экспериментируешь. Если понравилось - заменяешь оригинал. Если нет - удаляешь копию.

**🎯 Цель урока:**
- Создать ветку
- Добавить новую фичу
- Безопасно слить в main

**🔗 Видео:** [Ветки в Git за 5 минут](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FyAAIHHClqI)

steps.name: Смотрим текущие ветки
steps.description:
👀 Посмотрим какие ветки у нас есть.

**Простыми словами:**
Сейчас у тебя одна ветка - main. Это основная ветка.

**Звёздочка (*) = где ты сейчас находишься**
steps.actions:
```bash
# Перейди в проект
cd ~/projects/my-portfolio

# Посмотри список веток
git branch
```

**Ты увидишь:**
```
* main
```

Зелёная звёздочка (*) показывает что ты в ветке main.

**Ещё полезные команды:**

```bash
# Посмотреть все ветки (включая удалённые)
git branch -a

# Посмотреть последний коммит в каждой ветке
git branch -v
```
steps.self_check:
✅ Команда `git branch` показывает * main
✅ Звёздочка зелёного цвета
✅ Понял что main это название ветки

steps.name: Fork чужого проекта
steps.description: Научимся вносить изменения в чужие проекты.
steps.actions:
**Найдём проект для вклада:**

1. Открой https://github.com/search?q=good+first+issue+label%3A%22good+first+issue%22
2. Или просто форкни учебный проект:
   https://github.com/example/awesome-project
3. Нажми **Fork** справа вверху
4. Выбери свой аккаунт
5. Проект скопировался к тебе!

```bash
# Склонируй СВОЙ форк
git clone https://github.com/твой-username/awesome-project.git
cd awesome-project

# Добавь оригинал как upstream
git remote add upstream https://github.com/example/awesome-project.git

# Проверь
git remote -v
```

```yaml
name: Validate HTML

on:

    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]

jobs:
  validate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      
      - name: Install validator
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y tidy
      
      - name: Validate HTML

          tidy -q -e index.html || true

EOF

#!/bin/bash
echo "🧪 Running tests..."

test -f style.css && echo "✅ style.css exists" || exit 1

# Проверка что есть title

echo "✅ All tests passed!"
```

Создай `.github/workflows/test.yml`:

```yaml
name: Tests

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:

      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          chmod +x test.sh

```

name: Deploy

on:

    types: [published]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      
      - name: Deploy notification
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying version ${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}"

      - name: Deploy to GitHub Pages
        uses: peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v3
        with:
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          publish_dir: ./
```

Создай `.github/workflows/matrix.yml`:

```yaml
name: Cross-platform Test

on: [push]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
        node: [16, 18, 20]
    
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      
      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v3
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node }}
      
      - name: Test
        run: |
          node --version
          echo "✅ Testing on ${{ matrix.os }} with Node ${{ matrix.node }}"
```

steps.name: Зависимости между jobs
steps.description: Настроим последовательное выполнение задач.
steps.actions:
Создай `.github/workflows/pipeline.yml`:

```yaml
name: CI/CD Pipeline

on: [push]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - name: Run tests
        run: ./test.sh
  
  build:
    needs: test  # Запустится только если test успешен
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - name: Build
        run: echo "Building..."
  
  deploy:
    needs: build  # Запустится после build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'  # Только для main
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - name: Deploy
        run: echo "Deploying to production..."
```

Последовательность: test → build → deploy

```bash
git add .github/workflows/pipeline.yml
git commit -m "Add CI/CD pipeline"
git push origin main
```

steps.name: Git Stash - отложенные изменения

steps.name: Git Rebase - переписываем историю
steps.description: Сделаем историю коммитов линейной и красивой.
steps.actions:
**Merge vs Rebase:** - Merge: создаёт merge коммит (история разветвлённая) - Rebase: переносит коммиты (история линейная) ```bash # Создай feature ветку git checkout -b feature-skills echo "

Skills

" >> index.html git add index.html git commit -m "Add skills header" echo "
  • Git
" >> index.html git add index.html git commit -m "Add skills list" # В это время в main что-то изменилось git checkout main echo "" >> index.html git add index.html git commit -m "Update main" # Вернись в feature git checkout feature-skills # Rebase на main git rebase main # Твои коммиты 'переехали' поверх main! git log --oneline --graph ```

steps.name: Интерактивный rebase
steps.description: Отредактируем историю: объединим, переименуем коммиты.
steps.actions:
```bash # Создай 3 'грязных' коммита git checkout -b feature-contact echo "

Email

" >> index.html git add index.html git commit -m "add email" echo "

Phone

" >> index.html git add index.html git commit -m "add phone oops typo" echo "

Address

" >> index.html git add index.html git commit -m "add address" # Отредактируй последние 3 коммита git rebase -i HEAD~3 ``` Откроется редактор: ``` pick abc123 add email pick def456 add phone oops typo pick ghi789 add address ``` Измени на: ``` pick abc123 add email reword def456 add phone oops typo # Переименуем squash ghi789 add address # Объединим с предыдущим ``` Сохрани → Отредактируй сообщения → Готово! ```bash git log --oneline # Теперь 2 красивых коммита вместо 3! ```

steps.name: Cherry-pick - выборочный перенос
steps.description: Перенесём один коммит из другой ветки.
steps.actions:
**Ситуация:** В feature ветке есть нужный bugfix, но вся ветка не готова.

```bash
# Создай feature с багфиксом
git checkout -b feature-experimental
echo "<!-- Bugfix -->" >> index.html
git add index.html
git commit -m "Fix critical bug"

echo "<!-- Experimental -->" >> index.html
git add index.html
git commit -m "Experimental feature"

# Скопируй SHA багфикса
git log --oneline
# abc1234 Experimental feature
# def5678 Fix critical bug  <- Этот нужен!

# Переключись в main
git checkout main

# Перенеси только bugfix
git cherry-pick def5678

# Багфикс в main, без экспериментальной фичи!
git log --oneline
```

steps.name: Git Hooks - автоматизация
steps.description: Настроим автоматические проверки перед коммитом.
steps.actions:
**Создадим pre-commit hook:**

```bash
cd ~/Documents/projects/my-portfolio

# Создай hook скрипт
cat > .git/hooks/pre-commit << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash

echo "🔍 Running pre-commit checks..."

# Проверка что нет TODO в коде
if git diff --cached | grep -i 'TODO'; then
  echo "❌ Found TODO in code! Fix before commit."
  exit 1
fi

# Проверка HTML
if [ -f index.html ]; then
  if ! grep -q "<!DOCTYPE html>" index.html; then
    echo "❌ Invalid HTML!"
    exit 1
  fi
fi

echo "✅ All checks passed!"
exit 0
EOF

# Сделай исполняемым
chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Тестируем
echo "<!-- TODO: fix this -->" >> index.html
git add index.html
git commit -m "Test"
# Коммит заблокирован! Hook сработал!
```
steps.self_check: Hook запускается перед каждым коммитом. Блокирует плохой код

steps.name: Husky - hooks для команды
steps.description: Настроим hooks которые работают у всей команды.
steps.actions:
**Проблема:** Hooks в .git/hooks/ не коммитятся.
**Решение:** Husky!

```bash
# Инициализируй npm проект
npm init -y

# Установи Husky
npm install husky --save-dev

# Инициализируй
npx husky init

# Создай pre-commit hook
cat > .husky/pre-commit << 'EOF'
#!/bin/sh
echo "🔍 Checking code..."

# Проверка файлов
if git diff --cached --name-only | grep -q ".html$"; then
  echo "✅ HTML files detected"
fi

echo "✅ Pre-commit checks passed!"
EOF

chmod +x .husky/pre-commit

# Закоммить
git add package.json .husky/
git commit -m "Add Husky hooks"

# Теперь hook работает у всех кто склонирует проект!
```
steps.self_check: Папка .husky/ в репозитории. Hooks работают после клонирования

steps.name: Git Bisect - поиск бага
steps.description: Найдём коммит который сломал код.
steps.actions:
**Ситуация:** Неделю назад всё работало, сейчас баг. Где сломалось?

```bash
# Создадим историю с багом
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "Version $i" >> index.html
  git add index.html
  git commit -m "Update $i"
done

# На 6 версии добавим баг
git checkout HEAD~4
echo "<script>alert('bug')</script>" >> index.html
git add index.html
git commit --amend --no-edit

# Вернёмся в main
git checkout main

# Запустим поиск
git bisect start
git bisect bad  # Сейчас плохо
git bisect good HEAD~10  # 10 коммитов назад было хорошо

# Git проверяет средний коммит
# Проверь код, скажи хороший или плохой:
git bisect good  # или bad

# Git сужает поиск пока не найдёт виновника!
# Завершение:
git bisect reset
```
steps.self_check: Git находит конкретный коммит который сломал функционал

steps.name: Reflog - время машина
steps.description: Восстановим потерянные коммиты.
steps.actions:
**Ситуация:** Случайно удалил ветку с важными коммитами!

```bash
# Создай ветку с работой
git checkout -b important-work
echo "Important data" > important.txt
git add important.txt
git commit -m "Important work"

# Скопируй SHA коммита
git log --oneline

# Случайно удаляем ветку!
git checkout main
git branch -D important-work
# Ветка удалена! Коммит потерян!

# НО Git помнит всё!
git reflog
# Найди строку: abc1234 HEAD@{1}: commit: Important work

# Восстанови коммит
git checkout abc1234
# Или создай новую ветку:
git checkout -b recovered-work abc1234

# Данные спасены!
```

steps.name: Подписание коммитов GPG
steps.description: Подтвердим что коммиты действительно от тебя.
steps.actions:
**Зачем:** Верифицированный значок ✅ на GitHub.

```bash
# Создай GPG ключ
gpg --full-generate-key
# Выбери: RSA, 4096, не истекает, введи имя и email

# Посмотри ключи
gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format LONG
# sec   rsa4096/ABCD1234 ...

# Экспортируй публичный ключ
gpg --armor --export ABCD1234

# Скопируй весь вывод (BEGIN - END)
# Добавь на GitHub: Settings → SSH and GPG keys → New GPG key

# Настрой Git
git config --global user.signingkey ABCD1234
git config --global commit.gpgsign true

# Теперь все коммиты подписаны!
git commit -m "Verified commit"

# На GitHub будет значок 'Verified' ✅
```

en.name: Git and GitHub: Practical Course
en.short_description: Build a real project from your first commit to auto-deploy
en.description:
## 🚀 Why this course?

You walk into an interview. The recruiter asks: *"Show me your GitHub"*. And you have... nothing.

**93% of employers** check GitHub before inviting candidates for interviews. Without an active profile your chances of getting an offer drop 3x.

## 💡 What you'll get

A **real portfolio** that works:
- 🌐 Live website on GitHub Pages
- 🔥 Commit history showing you can work with Git
- ✅ Automated tests and CI/CD
- 🎯 Pull Requests and Code Reviews in your portfolio
- 📊 A GitHub profile that impresses

## 📚 Curriculum

5 hands-on lessons from simple to advanced:

1️⃣ **Git Basics** — First repo, commits, deploy
2️⃣ **Branches & merge** — Feature branches, conflicts
3️⃣ **Team collaboration** — Fork, Pull Requests, Code Review
4️⃣ **Automation** — GitHub Actions, CI/CD, auto-tests
5️⃣ **Pro level** — Rebase, cherry-pick, hooks, bisect

**No boring lectures** — only practice!

lessons.name: Day 1: Your First GitHub Project
lessons.short_description: 📱 Alex decided to become a developer. First step — create your portfolio

steps.name: Meet the Hero
steps.description:
👋 Meet Alex — he's learning to become a developer. Today we'll create your first portfolio on GitHub together!

**🎯 Goal:** Create your first GitHub project

**📚 What you'll learn:**
- What Git is and why you need it
- How to create a GitHub account
- How to publish your first site online

**⏱️ Time:** 30-40 minutes
steps.self_check: You understand why GitHub matters and are ready to start
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ If something's not working:**

- **Restart your terminal**
  - Close and open it again
  - Some changes only apply after restart
  
- **Clear browser cache**
  - Press Ctrl+Shift+R (Windows/Linux)
  - Cmd+Shift+R (Mac)
  
- **Check all commands were executed in order**
  - Go back one step
  - Carefully redo everything
self_check_items: I understood the material in this step

steps.name: Install Git
steps.description:
🌐 Create your professional GitHub profile. Think of it as a social network for developers — employers check it before interviews.

**💡 Tip:** Choose a username you'd be comfortable showing employers.
- ✅ Good: alex-petrov, maria-dev, ivan-codes
- ❌ Bad: killer228, mega_hacker
steps.actions:
**Registration steps:**

1. 🌐 Go to https://github.com

2. 📝 Click the big green **Sign up** button

3. ✉️ Enter your real email (you'll receive a confirmation)

4. 👤 Choose a username (this will be your URL)

5. 🔐 Create a password (minimum 8 characters)

6. 🤖 Complete the 'I'm not a robot' verification

7. ✅ Click **Create account**

8. 📧 Check your email and click the confirmation link

9. 📸 **Important:** Set up your profile:
   - Settings → Profile
   - Upload a photo (or avatar)
   - Fill in Name (your real name)
   - Write Bio: 'Junior developer 💻'

**⏱️ You can skip the GitHub survey**
steps.self_check: Profile at github.com/your-username is visible with a photo and confirmed email
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Registration problems:**

- **Email didn't arrive?**
  - Check your Spam folder
  - Try a different email (Gmail, Outlook)
  - Wait 5 minutes - sometimes emails are delayed

- **Username taken?**
  - Add numbers: `alex-dev-2024`
  - Add your specialization: `alex-frontend`
  - Use hyphens instead of underscores

- **Password rejected?**
  - Minimum 8 characters
  - Must include letters AND numbers
  - Example: `MyPass2024`
self_check_items: Go to github.com/your-username - see your page
You have a photo and name

steps.name: Configure Git
steps.description: 🎨 Tell Git who you are. This name and email will appear on all your commits and be visible on GitHub.
steps.actions:
**Open your terminal and run 2 commands:**

```bash
# Replace 'Alex Petrov' with your name
git config --global user.name "Alex Petrov"

# Replace with your GitHub email
git config --global user.email "alex@example.com"
```

**❗ Important:** Email must EXACTLY match your GitHub email!

**Verification:**
```bash
# Check what was saved
git config user.name
git config user.email
```

Should display your data.
steps.self_check: `git config user.name` shows your name, `git config user.email` shows your GitHub email
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Git config issues:**

- **Command not found?**
  - Make sure Git is installed: `git --version`
  - Restart terminal
  
- **Config not saving?**
  - Check quotes: use double quotes ""
  - Remove --global flag to set only for current project
  
- **Shows wrong email?**
  - Re-run the command with correct email
  - Must match your GitHub email exactly
self_check_items: Run `git config user.name` - shows your name
Run `git config user.email` - shows GitHub email

steps.name: Create a Repository
steps.description:
📦 A repository = a folder for your project on GitHub. We'll create `my-portfolio` — your future career showcase.

**🎯 After this:** You'll have a link: github.com/your-username/my-portfolio
steps.actions:
**Step-by-step:**

1. 🏠 Go to https://github.com (you must be logged in)

2. ➕ Find the green **New** button (top left)
   - Or navigate to: https://github.com/new

3. 📝 Fill in the form:
   
   **Repository name:** `my-portfolio`
   
   **Description:** `My first project - portfolio website 🌟`
   
   **Choose:** 🌍 **Public** (so everyone can see it)
   
   **✅ Check:** Add a README file
   
4. 🎉 Click the green **Create repository** button

5. 🎊 Congrats! You created your first repository!

**What you see:**
- README.md file (project description)
- Address: github.com/your-username/my-portfolio
steps.self_check: Repository page open, README.md visible, URL: github.com/username/my-portfolio, green 'Code' button visible
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Can't create repository:**

- **Name taken?**
  - Add a number: `my-portfolio-2024`
  - Use hyphens: `my-first-portfolio`
  
- **New button inactive?**
  - Check you're logged in (profile icon top right)
  - Try: https://github.com/new
  
- **Can't verify email?**
  - Settings → Emails → Resend verification

steps.name: Clone the Project
steps.description: 💾 Clone = download the project from GitHub to your computer so you can edit files locally and then push changes back.
steps.actions:
**Step 1: Copy the link**

1. Open your repository on GitHub
2. Click the green **Code** button
3. Select the **HTTPS** tab
4. Click 📋 (copy)
5. Link looks like: `https://github.com/username/my-portfolio.git`

**Step 2: Clone in terminal**

```bash
# Create a projects folder
mkdir ~/projects
cd ~/projects

# Paste your link instead of USERNAME
git clone https://github.com/USERNAME/my-portfolio.git

# Navigate into the project
cd my-portfolio

# Check the contents
ls
```

**What you'll see:**
```
README.md
```

✅ Perfect! Project cloned!
steps.self_check: Folder `my-portfolio` created with README.md inside, `ls` command works
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Cloning problems:**

- **"Permission denied"**
  - Check repository is Public
  - Use HTTPS link (not SSH)
  
- **Folder not created**
  - Check you're in the right folder: `pwd`
  - Create folder manually: `mkdir ~/projects`
  
- **"Already exists"**
  - Delete old folder: `rm -rf my-portfolio`
  - Or use a different name

steps.name: Create an HTML Page
steps.description: 🎨 Create `index.html` — your portfolio site's homepage. Start simple: DOCTYPE, your name, and a short intro. Open it in the browser to see the result.
steps.actions:
**Open an editor:** 🔹 VS Code (recommended) - https://code.visualstudio.com 🔹 Sublime Text 🔹 Notepad **Create index.html:** 1. Open the my-portfolio folder 2. Create a new file: index.html 3. Paste this code: ```html My Portfolio

👋 Hi! I'm Alex

Junior developer

``` 4. Replace 'Alex' with your name 5. Save (Ctrl+S) 6. Open in browser (double-click the file)
steps.self_check: index.html created, opens in browser, shows your name
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ HTML problems:**

- **Page doesn't open**
  - Check filename: exactly `index.html`?
  - File in correct folder?
  - Try opening via browser: File → Open
  
- **See nothing**
  - Check file is saved (Ctrl+S)
  - Refresh page (Ctrl+R or F5)
  
- **Weird encoding (gibberish)**
  - Check `<meta charset="UTF-8">` in code
self_check_items: Your name is visible on page

steps.name: First Commit
steps.description: 💾 Save the basic HTML structure. A commit = a snapshot of your project at this moment in time. Every meaningful change deserves its own commit.
steps.actions:
```bash
cd ~/projects/my-portfolio

# See what changed
git status

# Add the file
git add index.html

# Commit with a message
git commit -m "Created basic HTML structure"

# View the history
git log --oneline
```
steps.self_check: `git status` shows 'nothing to commit', `git log` shows the commit
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Commit problems:**

- **"nothing to commit"**
  - First add files: `git add .`
  - Check status: `git status`
  
- **"Please tell me who you are"**
  - Set name: `git config --global user.name "Your Name"`
  - Set email: `git config --global user.email "email@example.com"`
  
- **VIM editor opened?**
  - Press `Esc`
  - Type `:q!`
  - Press Enter
self_check_items: Commit message is visible in history

steps.name: Add Styles
steps.description: 🎨 Make the page beautiful: gradient background, centered card layout, modern system font. Add CSS inside a `<style>` tag in `<head>`.
steps.self_check: Purple gradient background, white card in center, text inside the card
steps.troubleshooting_help: **❌ Styles not working:** - **Nothing changed?** - Save file: Ctrl+S (Cmd+S on Mac) - Hard refresh: Ctrl+Shift+R - Check filename is exactly `index.html` - **Styles partially applied?** - Open DevTools: F12 - Check Console for errors - Verify `

steps.name: Commit the Styles
steps.description: 💾 Save the design changes with a descriptive commit message.
steps.actions:
```bash
# Check the changes
git diff index.html

# Add
git add index.html

# Commit
git commit -m "Added styles and gradient background"
```
steps.self_check: `git log` shows 2 commits
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Can't commit:**

- **"nothing to commit"**
  - Check file is saved: Ctrl+S
  - Try: `git add .`
  - Verify changes: `git status`
  
- **Wrong commit message?**
  - Amend last commit: `git commit --amend -m "New message"`
  
- **Forgot to add files?**
  - Add them: `git add index.html`
  - Commit again

steps.name: Add an Avatar
steps.description: 😊 Add a round emoji avatar above the heading to make your card more personal and visual.
steps.self_check: Round avatar with emoji and gradient background visible on the page
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Avatar problems:**

- **Avatar not round?**
  - Check `border-radius: 50%` in CSS
  - Verify width = height
  
- **Emoji not centered?**
  - Check `display: flex` properties
  - Try different emoji
  
- **No gradient?**
  - Verify `background: linear-gradient()` syntax
  - Check browser supports gradients

steps.name: Commit the Avatar
steps.description: 💾 Save the avatar addition.
steps.actions:
```bash
git add index.html
git commit -m "Added round avatar with emoji"
```
steps.self_check: `git log` shows 3 commits
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Can't commit:**

- **Changes not staged?**
  - Run: `git add index.html`
  - Then commit
  
- **Still shows 2 commits?**
  - Check you made changes
  - Verify file is saved
  - Try: `git status`

steps.name: Add Your Info
steps.description:
📝 Replace the placeholder `

` with a proper bio section: role, learning goals, and interests — each line with an emoji icon.
steps.self_check: Your info is visible, each line has an emoji icon
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Info not showing:** - **Text on one line?** - Check `
` tags are present - Verify HTML not broken - **Styles not applied?** - Check class names match - `.subtitle` and `.about` in CSS? - **Emojis not showing?** - Use different emojis - Check UTF-8 encoding

steps.name: Commit Your Info
steps.description: 💾 Save your personal info section.
steps.actions:
```bash
git add index.html
git commit -m "Added detailed personal information"
```
steps.self_check: 4 commits in history
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Commit issues:**

- **Only 3 commits?**
  - Check file is saved
  - Verify changes made
  - Try: `git status`
  
- **Wrong message?**
  - Amend: `git commit --amend -m "New message"`

steps.name: Add Skills
steps.description: ✨ Add a row of skill badges: HTML ✅, CSS ✅, Git ✅, JavaScript 📚. Use flex layout for the tags.
steps.self_check: Skills displayed as rounded badge tags, checkmarks on learned ones
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Skills problems:**

- **Skills on separate lines?**
  - Check `display: flex` in `.skills`
  - Verify `gap: 10px` present
  
- **Not centered?**
  - Check `justify-content: center`
  
- **Badges not rounded?**
  - Verify `border-radius: 20px` in `.skill`

steps.name: Commit the Skills
steps.description: 💾 Save the skills section.
steps.actions:
```bash
git add index.html
git commit -m "Added skills section"
```
steps.self_check: 5 commits in history with meaningful messages
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Commit issues:**

- **Still 4 commits?**
  - File saved?
  - Changes staged? `git add .`
  
- **Commit message unclear?**
  - Use descriptive messages
  - Format: "Added [feature]" or "Fixed [issue]"

steps.name: Push All Commits to GitHub
steps.description: ☁️ Upload all 5 commits to GitHub with `git push origin main`. If prompted for credentials, use a personal access token (not your password) from github.com/settings/tokens.
steps.actions:
```bash
git push origin main
```

⏳ **Git is pushing...**

**If asked for authentication:**

🔐 Username: your-github-username
🔑 Password: NOT your password! You need a token!

---

### 🎫 Creating a token (if needed):

1. https://github.com/settings/tokens
2. **Generate new token** → **Classic**
3. Note: `My computer`
4. Expiration: 90 days
5. Scopes: ✅ **repo** (all checkboxes)
6. **Generate token**
7. 📋 **COPY** and save it!
8. Paste instead of password

---

✅ **Success:**
```
   abc1234..xyz5678  main -> main
```
steps.self_check: Push successful, all 5 commits visible on GitHub
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Can't push:**

- **"Permission denied"**
  - Use Personal Access Token (Settings → Developer settings)
  - Token needs 'repo' scope
  - Use token as password, NOT account password
  
- **"Repository not found"**
  - Check remote URL: `git remote -v`
  - Fix URL: `git remote set-url origin <your-URL>`
  
- **"Non-fast-forward" error**
  - Pull changes first: `git pull origin main --rebase`
  - Then try push again

steps.name: Check the History on GitHub
steps.description: 📜 Click 'commits' on your repository page. See each commit, its message, and exactly what changed (green = added, red = removed). This is the power of Git!
steps.actions:
1. Open your repository on GitHub
2. Click on **5 commits** (top, left of the green Code button)
3. You'll see all 5 commits with descriptions!
4. Click on any commit - see exactly what changed
5. Green lines = added
6. Red lines = removed

**This is the magic of Git!** All history is saved!
steps.self_check: 5 commits visible with descriptions, can click any to see the diff
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Can't see commits:**

- **Push not completed?**
  - Check: `git push origin main`
  - Verify no errors
  
- **Commits show author 'unknown'?**
  - Set git config:
  - `git config --global user.name "Your Name"`
  - `git config --global user.email "email@example.com"`
  
- **Can't see diff?**
  - Click on commit hash
  - Green = additions, Red = deletions
self_check_items: Can click commits to see changes

steps.name: Publish the Site — Add Contacts
steps.description:
♻️ Add a contacts section with your GitHub and email links, commit, and push. GitHub Pages auto-updates within 1-2 minutes.

**Enable Pages:** Settings → Pages → Source: main, /(root) → Save.
steps.actions:
Open index.html, add before `` (closing tag of card): ```html

📧 Contact me:

💻 ✉️
``` Replace username and email! ```bash git add index.html git commit -m "Added contacts section" git push origin main ``` ⏳ Wait 30-60 seconds, refresh the site!
steps.self_check: Contacts added, committed and pushed, section visible on the live GitHub Pages site
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ GitHub Pages not working:**

- **404 error?**
  - Wait 2-3 minutes for deployment
  - Check: Settings → Pages → Source = main, /(root)
  - Verify `index.html` in root folder (not subfolder)
  
- **Site shows old version?**
  - Clear browser cache: Ctrl+Shift+R
  - Check push succeeded: `git log origin/main`
  
- **No styling?**
  - Check `<link href="style.css">` path
  - All files pushed: `git status`
self_check_items: GitHub Pages enabled in repository Settings
Contacts section visible on live site

steps.name: Lesson 1 Summary — Submit for Review 🎉
steps.description:
🏆 Congratulations! You completed Day 1!

**What you did:**
✅ Installed Git and created GitHub
✅ Created your first repository
✅ Built a portfolio page
✅ Made 6 meaningful commits
✅ Pushed to GitHub
✅ Published a live site!

**To complete this lesson:**
1. Copy your repository link: `https://github.com/your-username/my-portfolio`
2. Paste it in the submission field below
3. Click 'Submit for Review'
steps.self_check: Site works at username.github.io/my-portfolio, 6 commits on GitHub, repository link copied
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Final checklist issues:**

- **Site not live?**
  - Settings → Pages → Enable
  - Wait 2-3 minutes
  - Try incognito mode
  
- **Missing commits?**
  - Check: `git log --oneline`
  - Push: `git push origin main`
  
- **Can't find repository link?**
  - Format: `https://github.com/username/my-portfolio`
  - Copy from browser address bar

lessons.name: Day 2: Working with Branches
lessons.short_description: 📊 Alex wants to add a new feature but fears breaking the site. Branches to the rescue!

steps.name: Day 2 Story
steps.description:
📖 Alex's friend suggests adding a projects section. But Alex is scared: what if it breaks the working site?

The answer: **branches**.

🌳 **Branch = a copy of code for experiments**
- `main` = stable version (site works)
- feature branch = experimental (safe to break)
- If the experiment works → merge into main
- If not → just delete the branch
steps.self_check: You understand: main = stable, feature = experimental

steps.name: View Current Branches
steps.description: 👀 Check which branches exist. Right now you have one — `main`. The asterisk (*) shows your current branch.
steps.actions:
```bash
# Navigate to your project
cd ~/projects/my-portfolio

# View the list of branches
git branch
```

**You'll see:**
```
* main
```

The green asterisk (*) shows you're in the main branch.

**Other useful commands:**

```bash
# View all branches (including remote)
git branch -a

# View the last commit in each branch
git branch -v
```
steps.self_check: `git branch` shows `* main` with a green asterisk

steps.name: Create a Branch
steps.description: Create and switch to a new feature branch specifically for the projects section. You're now working in parallel to main.
steps.actions:
```bash
# Create and switch to the branch
git checkout -b feature-projects

# Verify
git branch
```

Now you're in the feature-projects branch!
steps.self_check: `git branch` shows `* feature-projects`
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Branch problems:**

- **"Branch already exists"**
  - Delete old: `git branch -d feature-projects`
  - Or switch to it: `git checkout feature-projects`
  
- **Can't switch branches?**
  - Commit or stash changes first: `git stash`
  - Then switch: `git checkout branch-name`
  
- **Wrong branch name?**
  - Rename: `git branch -m old-name new-name`
self_check_items: Can create and edit files normally

steps.name: Add a Projects Section
steps.description: Add a new projects div to index.html inside the feature branch. Save and open in browser to verify — main is untouched.
steps.actions:
Open index.html and add before ``: ```html

🚀 My Projects

Project 1: Portfolio Site

My first project with Git

GitHub →
``` Save and open in browser.
steps.self_check: Page shows 'My Projects' section in the browser

steps.name: Commit in the Branch
steps.description: 💾 Save the changes inside feature-projects branch. After this the branches have officially diverged.
steps.actions:
```bash
# Verify you're in the right branch
git branch

# Add and commit
git add index.html
git commit -m "Added projects section"

# View the history
git log --oneline --graph --all
```
steps.self_check: `git log --graph` shows branches have diverged

steps.name: Switch Between Branches
steps.description: ✨ See the magic! When you switch to `main`, the projects section disappears. Switch back to `feature-projects` — it's there again. Files physically change!
steps.actions:
```bash
# Currently in feature - section exists
cat index.html | grep "Projects"

# Switch to main
git checkout main

# Section is gone!
cat index.html | grep "Projects"

# Switch back
git checkout feature-projects

# Section is back!
```

Magic! The file physically changes when you switch branches!
steps.self_check: main has no projects section, feature-projects has it

steps.name: Merge the Branch
steps.description: Switch to main and run `git merge feature-projects` to bring the new section into the stable branch.
steps.actions:
```bash
# Switch to main
git checkout main

# Merge the feature branch
git merge feature-projects

# Check the file - section is now in main!
cat index.html | grep "Projects"

# Viewthe history
git log --oneline
```
steps.self_check: Projects section now in main. `git log` shows the commit from feature branch
steps.troubleshooting_help:
**❌ Merge problems:**

- **"Already up to date"**
  - Check you committed in feature branch
  - View branch history: `git log feature-projects`
  
- **Merge conflict?**
  - Open conflicted files
  - Remove `<<<<<<<`, `=======`, `>>>>>>>` markers
  - Keep desired code, `git add`, `git commit`
  
- **Want to abort merge?**
  - `git merge --abort` to cancel
self_check_items: Feature branch code is in main

steps.name: Delete the Branch
steps.description: The branch served its purpose — delete it to keep the repo clean, then push changes to GitHub.
steps.actions:
```bash
# Delete the feature branch
git branch -d feature-projects

# Verify
git branch

# Push changes to GitHub
git push origin main
```
steps.self_check: `git branch` shows only `main`

steps.name: Create a PR on GitHub
steps.description: Push a new feature branch to GitHub, then open a Pull Request: Compare & pull request → fill in description → Create. This is the standard team workflow.
steps.actions:
```bash
# Create a new branch
git checkout -b feature-contacts

# Add contacts to index.html
# ... (add a contacts section with email and GitHub)

# Commit and push
git add index.html
git commit -m "Added contacts"
git push origin feature-contacts
```

Now on GitHub:
1. Pull requests → New pull request
2. base: main, compare: feature-contacts  
3. Create pull request
4. Fill in the description
5. Create
steps.self_check: PR created and visible in Pull requests on GitHub

steps.name: Review and Merge the PR
steps.description: Open the PR, click Files changed to see the diff. Leave a comment if needed. Then Merge pull request → Confirm. Update local main with `git pull`.
steps.actions:
1. Open the PR on GitHub
2. Files changed - view the diff
3. You can leave a comment
4. Merge pull request → Confirm
5. Delete branch (optional)

```bash
# Locally update main
git checkout main
git pull origin main
```
steps.self_check: PR merged. Local main is up to date after `git pull`

steps.name: Resolve Merge Conflicts
steps.description: Create a deliberate conflict (edit the same line in both branches), attempt merge, read the `<<<<<<<` / `=======` / `>>>>>>>` markers, pick the correct version, remove markers, and complete the merge.
steps.actions:
**Create a conflict:** ```bash # In main change the heading git checkout main # In index.html:

Hi! I'm a programmer

git add index.html git commit -m "Updated heading" # In new branch change the same heading git checkout -b feature-header # In index.html:

👨‍💻 Hi! I'm a developer

git add index.html git commit -m "Added emoji" # Try to merge git checkout main git merge feature-header # CONFLICT! ``` **Resolve:** 1. Open index.html - you'll see markers: ``` <<<<<<< HEAD

Hi! I'm a programmer

=======

👨‍💻 Hi! I'm a developer

>>>>>>> feature-header ``` 2. Choose the version you want, remove markers: ```html

👨‍💻 Hi! I'm a programmer

``` 3. Complete the merge: ```bash git add index.html git commit -m "Resolved conflict" ```
steps.self_check: Conflict resolved, merge completed, no conflict markers in the file

lessons.name: Team Collaboration
lessons.short_description: Fork, Pull Requests, Code Review — work like a real development team

steps.name: Fork a Project
steps.description: Learn to contribute to other people's projects. Fork = copy the project to your own account. Then clone your fork locally and add the original as `upstream` remote.
steps.actions:
**Find a project to contribute to:**

1. Go to https://github.com/search?q=good+first+issue+label%3A%22good+first+issue%22
2. Or just fork a tutorial project:
   https://github.com/example/awesome-project
3. Click **Fork** (top right)
4. Select your account
5. The project is now copied to your account!

```bash
# Clone YOUR fork
git clone https://github.com/your-username/awesome-project.git
cd awesome-project

# Add the original as upstream
git remote add upstream https://github.com/example/awesome-project.git

# Verify
git remote -v
```
steps.self_check: `git remote -v` shows both `origin` (your fork) and `upstream` (original)

steps.name: Sync with the Original
steps.description: Keep your fork up to date: `git fetch upstream` → `git merge upstream/main` → `git push origin main`. Do this before every new feature!
steps.actions:
```bash
# Fetch changes from the original
git fetch upstream

# Switch to main
git checkout main

# Merge changes
git merge upstream/main

# Push to your fork
git push origin main
```

**Do this regularly before starting new features!**
steps.self_check: Your fork shows 'This branch is up to date with upstream:main'

steps.name: Make an Improvement
steps.description: Create a feature branch, fix a real issue (e.g. a typo in README), commit with a clear message, push to your fork.
steps.actions:
```bash
# Create a branch for your fix
git checkout -b fix-typo-in-readme

# Find a typo in README and fix it
# For example: 'instalation' → 'installation'

# Commit
git add README.md
git commit -m "Fix typo: instalation → installation"

# Push to YOUR fork
git push origin fix-typo-in-readme
```
steps.self_check: Branch appeared in your fork on GitHub

steps.name: Create a Pull Request
steps.description: Go to your fork on GitHub. Click 'Compare & pull request'. Set base = original repo, compare = your branch. Write a clear description of the change. Submit.
steps.actions:
1. Go to YOUR fork on GitHub
2. You'll see a notification 'Compare & pull request'
3. Click on it
4. **base repository**: example/awesome-project (original)
5. **head repository**: your-username/awesome-project
6. Title: 'Fix typo in README'
7. Description:
```
Fixed a typo in installation instructions:
- instalation → installation
```
8. Create pull request
steps.self_check: PR created and visible in the original repository's Pull requests

steps.name: Code Review: Request Changes
steps.description: Practice code review on your own project: create a PR with 'bad' code (inline styles), comment on it, request changes, fix locally, push — the PR updates automatically.
steps.actions:
Back to my-portfolio:

```bash
cd ~/projects/my-portfolio

# Create a branch with 'bad' code
git checkout -b feature-bad-code
```

Add to index.html:
```html
<style>
  /* bad code: global styles */
  div { background: red; }
</style>
```

```bash
git add index.html
git commit -m "Added styles"
git push origin feature-bad-code
```

**On GitHub:**
1. Create a PR
2. Leave yourself a comment:
   'Styles should be in a separate CSS file'
3. Request changes
4. Fix it locally
5. Push to the same branch - PR updates automatically!
steps.self_check: PR shows your review comments and the updated code

steps.name: Work with Issues
steps.description: Create an issue describing a bug or feature. Reference it in a commit with `Fixes #1`. After the PR merges, GitHub automatically closes the issue.
steps.actions:
**In my-portfolio on GitHub:**

1. Issues → New issue
2. Title: 'Add responsive design'
3. Description:
```
Site doesn't look good on mobile.
TODO:
- [ ] Add meta viewport
- [ ] Make container flexible
- [ ] Test on phone
```
4. Submit
5. Copy the issue number (#1)

**Locally:**
```bash
git checkout -b feature-responsive
# Make changes
git commit -m "Add responsive design. Fixes #1"
git push origin feature-responsive
```

**GitHub will auto-close the issue when the PR merges!**
steps.self_check: Issue created. Commit references it with 'Fixes #N'. Issue closes after merge

steps.name: Protect the main Branch
steps.description: Settings → Branches → Add rule → Require pull request before merging + Require approvals. Now direct push to main is blocked.
steps.actions:
**In repository settings:**

1. Settings → Branches
2. Add branch protection rule
3. Branch name pattern: `main`
4. Enable:
   - ✅ Require pull request before merging
   - ✅ Require approvals (1)
5. Save

**Now try:**
```bash
git checkout main
echo "test" >> README.md
git add README.md
git commit -m "Test"
git push origin main
# ERROR! Can't push to main!
```

**The correct way:**
Only through PR with approval.
steps.self_check: Direct push to main is blocked with 'protected branch' error

steps.name: GitHub Projects
steps.description: Create a Kanban-style project board: New project → Board template → columns: 📋 Backlog, 🚧 In Progress, ✅ Done. Add cards and drag them between columns.
steps.actions:
1. In my-portfolio → Projects → New project
2. Choose 'Board' template
3. Name: 'Portfolio Development'
4. Create columns:
   - 📋 Backlog
   - 🚧 In Progress
   - ✅ Done
5. Add cards:
   - 'Add Skills section'
   - 'Make dark theme'
   - 'Add animations'
6. Drag cards between columns!
7. You can convert them to Issues
steps.self_check: Project board created, cards can be dragged between columns

steps.name: GitHub Wiki
steps.description: Create project documentation in the Wiki tab. Add a Home page with setup instructions, project structure, and deployment info. Add a second page 'Development Guide'.
steps.actions:
1. In my-portfolio → Wiki → Create the first page
2. Title: 'Home'
3. Content:
```markdown
# Project Documentation

git clone https://github.com/username/my-portfolio.git

- `script.js` - scripts

## Deployment
Auto-deployed via GitHub Pages.
```
4. Save
5. Create another page 'Development Guide'
steps.self_check: Wiki accessible with at least 2 pages

steps.name: Collaboration
steps.description: Add a collaborator (Settings → Collaborators). Simulate teamwork: you work on feature-nav branch, friend works on feature-footer. Create PRs and review each other's code.
steps.actions:
1. Settings → Collaborators → Add people
2. Enter friend's username (or create a second GitHub account)
3. Send invitation

**Simulate teamwork:**

```bash
# You (developer 1)
git checkout -b feature-nav
# Add navigation
git commit -m "Add navigation"
git push origin feature-nav
# Create PR

git checkout -b feature-footer

```

steps.name: What is GitHub Actions
steps.description:
GitHub Actions = automatedasks triggered by events.

Examples:
- On push → run tests
- On PR → check code quality
- On merge → deploy site

Workflow file structure:
```yaml
name: My Workflow
on: [push]
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Say hello
        run: echo 'Hello!'
```
steps.actions:
**GitHub Actions = automatic tasks triggered by events.**

Examples:
- On push → run tests
- On PR → check code
- On merge → deploy site

**Structure:**
```yaml
name: My Workflow
on: [push, pull_request]  # when to trigger
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - name: Step 1
        run: echo "Hello"
```
steps.self_check: You understand the concept: event → workflow → steps → result

steps.name: First Workflow
steps.description: Create `.github/workflows/greet.yml` that runs on every push and prints a greeting. Push to GitHub and watch it run in the Actions tab.
steps.actions:
```bash
cd ~/projects/my-portfolio

# Create the structure
mkdir -p .github/workflows

# Create the file
cat > .github/workflows/greet.yml << 'EOF'
name: Greet

on: [push]

jobs:
  greet:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Say hello
        run: echo "Hello from GitHub Actions!"
EOF

# Commit and push
git add .github/
git commit -m "Add first GitHub Action"
git push origin main
```

**On GitHub:**
Actions → you'll see the workflow running!
steps.self_check: A successful 'Greet' workflow with green checkmark appears in Actions

steps.name: HTML Validation
steps.description: Add `.github/workflows/validate.yml` that installs `tidy` and validates your HTML on every push to main and every PR.
steps.actions:
Create `.github/workflows/validate.yml`:

```yaml
name: Validate HTML

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]

jobs:
  validate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      
      - name: Install validator
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y tidy
      
      - name: Validate HTML
        run: |
          tidy -q -e index.html || true
          echo "✅ HTML validation complete"
```

```bash
# Commit
git add .github/workflows/validate.yml
git commit -m "Add HTML validation"
git push origin main
```
steps.self_check: Validation workflow runs on every push and checks the HTML

steps.name: Automated Testing
steps.description: Write `test.sh` (check files exist, DOCTYPE present, title present). Create `.github/workflows/test.yml` to run it on every push and PR.
steps.actions:
Create `test.sh`:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
echo "🧪 Running tests..."

# Check files exist
test -f index.html && echo "✅ index.html exists" || exit 1
test -f style.css && echo "✅ style.css exists" || exit 1

# Check valid HTML
grep -q "<!DOCTYPE html>" index.html && echo "✅ Valid HTML" || exit 1

# Check title exists
grep -q "<title>" index.html && echo "✅ Title exists" || exit 1

echo "✅ All tests passed!"
```

Create `.github/workflows/test.yml`:

```yaml
name: Tests

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      
      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          chmod +x test.sh
          ./test.sh
```

```bash
git add test.sh .github/workflows/test.yml
git commit -m "Add automated tests"
git push origin main
```
steps.self_check: Tests run and pass (green checkmark in Actions)

steps.name: Status Badges
steps.description: Add workflow status badges to the top of README.md so anyone who opens your repo can see the build and test status at a glance.
steps.actions:
Open README.md and add at the top:

```markdown
# My Portfolio

![Tests](https://github.com/username/my-portfolio/workflows/Tests/badge.svg)
![HTML Validation](https://github.com/username/my-portfolio/workflows/Validate%20HTML/badge.svg)
![GitHub Pages](https://img.shields.io/badge/demo-live-success)

> My portfolio site with automated tests

[🌍 Demo](https://username.github.io/my-portfolio/)
```

```bash
git add README.md
git commit -m "Add status badges"
git push origin main
```
steps.self_check: Green status badges visible in README on GitHub

steps.name: Auto-deploy on Release
steps.description: Create `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` triggered by `release: published`. Use `peaceiris/actions-gh-pages` to deploy. Then create a Release (v1.0.0) and watch it auto-deploy.
steps.actions:
Create `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`:

```yaml
name: Deploy

on:
  release:
    types: [published]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      
      - name: Deploy notification
        run: |
          echo "🚀 Deploying version ${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}"
          echo "📝 Release notes:"
          echo "${{ github.event.release.body }}"
      
      - name: Deploy to GitHub Pages
        uses: peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v3
        with:
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          publish_dir: ./
```

```bash
git add .github/workflows/deploy.yml
git commit -m "Add deploy workflow"
git push origin main
```

**Create a release:**
1. Releases → Create new release
2. Tag: v1.0.0
3. Title: First Release
4. Description: Initial version with CI/CD
5. Publish

Workflow runs automatically!
steps.self_check: Deploy workflow runs automatically after publishing a release

steps.name: Scheduled Tasks (Cron)
steps.description: Create `.github/workflows/scheduled.yml` with cron `0 9 * * *` (daily at 9 UTC) plus `workflow_dispatch` for manual runs. Test it by clicking 'Run workflow' in Actions.
steps.actions:
Create `.github/workflows/scheduled.yml`:

```yaml
name: Daily Check

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 9 * * *'  # Every day at 9:00 UTC
  workflow_dispatch:  # Can trigger manually

jobs:
  check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      
      - name: Check site health
        run: |
          echo "🏥 Checking site health..."
          # Check files exist
          test -f index.html && echo "✅ Site files OK"
          
          # Can check if site is accessible
          curl -f https://username.github.io/my-portfolio/ || echo "⚠️ Site down"
```

```bash
git add .github/workflows/scheduled.yml
git commit -m "Add scheduled checks"
git push origin main
```

**Test:**
Actions → Daily Check → Run workflow
steps.self_check: Workflow can be triggered manually from Actions tab

steps.name: Matrix Testing
steps.description: Use `strategy.matrix` to run your tests on 3 OS (ubuntu, macos, windows) × 3 Node.js versions (16, 18, 20) = 9 parallel jobs in one push.
steps.actions:
Create `.github/workflows/matrix.yml`:

```yaml
name: Cross-platform Test

on: [push]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
        node: [16, 18, 20]
    
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      
      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v3
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node }}
      
      - name: Test
        run: |
          node --version
          echo "✅ Testing on ${{ matrix.os }} with Node ${{ matrix.node }}"
```

This runs 9 tests (3 OS × 3 Node versions)!

```bash
git add .github/workflows/matrix.yml
git commit -m "Add matrix testing"
git push origin main
```
steps.self_check: 9 parallel test jobs run on different OS/Node combinations

steps.name: Secrets and Variables
steps.description: Add a secret (Settings → Secrets → New repository secret). Use it in a workflow with `${{ secrets.MY_SECRET }}`. It will show as *** in logs — never exposed.
steps.actions:
**Add secrets:**

1. Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions
2. New repository secret:
   - Name: `TELEGRAM_TOKEN`
   - Value: your token
3. New variable:
   - Name: `DEPLOY_ENV`
   - Value: production

**Use in workflow:**

```yaml
name: Use Secrets

on: [push]

jobs:
  notify:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Send notification
        run: |
          echo "Environment: ${{ vars.DEPLOY_ENV }}"
          # Secret is NOT shown in logs!
          curl -X POST https://api.telegram.org/bot${{ secrets.TELEGRAM_TOKEN }}/sendMessage \
            -d chat_id=123456 \
            -d text="Deploy complete!"
```

**Important:** Secrets are never shown in logs!
steps.self_check: Secret created. Value shows as *** in workflow logs

steps.name: Job Dependencies
steps.description: Use `needs:` to chain jobs: `test` → `build` (needs: test) → `deploy` (needs: build, only on main). Jobs run sequentially and each only if the previous succeeded.
steps.actions:
Create `.github/workflows/pipeline.yml`:

```yaml
name: CI/CD Pipeline

on: [push]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - name: Run tests
        run: ./test.sh
  
  build:
    needs: test  # Runs only if test succeeds
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - name: Build
        run: echo "Building..."
  
  deploy:
    needs: build  # Runs after build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'  # Only for main
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - name: Deploy
        run: echo "Deploying to production..."
```

Sequence: test → build → deploy

```bash
git add .github/workflows/pipeline.yml
git commit -m "Add CI/CD pipeline"
git push origin main
```
steps.self_check: Jobs run in sequence. Deploy only triggers for the main branch

lessons.name: Advanced Techniques
lessons.short_description: git stash, rebase, cherry-pick, hooks — professional-level tools

steps.name: Git Stash — Save Work in Progress
steps.description:
**Scenario:** You're coding but need to urgently switch to a hotfix.

`git stash` shelves your uncommitted changes (file returns to clean state). `git stash pop` restores them. You can maintain multiple stashes.
steps.actions:
**Scenario:** You're coding but need to urgently switch to a hotfix. ```bash cd ~/projects/my-portfolio # Start making changes echo "

Work in progress...

" >> index.html # Check status git status # You have changes but they're not ready to commit # Stash the changes git stash # File is back to original state! cat index.html # View what's in stash git stash list # Switch to hotfix git checkout -b hotfix-urgent # Do the hotfix... # Go back to your work git checkout main # Restore changes from stash git stash pop # Your work continues! ```
steps.self_check: `git stash list` shows saved changes. They are restored after `git stash pop`

steps.name: Named Stashes
steps.description: Give stashes meaningful names with `git stash save "WIP: navigation"`. View with `git stash list`. Apply a specific one with `git stash apply stash@{1}`. Drop or clear as needed.
steps.actions:
```bash
# Change navigation
echo "<nav>Menu</nav>" >> index.html
git stash save "WIP: navigation redesign"

# Click footer
echo "<footer>Footer</footer>" >> index.html
git stash save "WIP: footer update"

# View the list
git stash list
# stash@{0}: WIP: footer update
# stash@{1}: WIP: navigation redesign

# Apply a specific stash
git stash apply stash@{1}

# Drop an unneeded one
git stash drop stash@{0}

# Clear all
git stash clear
```
steps.self_check: Can create multiple named stashes and apply them selectively

steps.name: Git Rebase — Rewrite History
steps.description:
**Merge vs Rebase:**
- Merge: creates an extra merge commit (branched history)
- Rebase: moves your commits on top of main (linear history)

Rebase makes the project history cleaner and easier to read.
steps.actions:
**Merge vs Rebase:** - Merge: creates a merge commit (branched history) - Rebase: moves commits (linear history) ```bash # Create a feature branch git checkout -b feature-skills echo "

Skills

" >> index.html git add index.html git commit -m "Add skills header" echo "
  • Git
" >> index.html git add index.html git commit -m "Add skills list" # Meanwhile in main something changed git checkout main echo "" >> index.html git add index.html git commit -m "Update main" # Go back to feature git checkout feature-skills # Rebase onto main git rebase main # Your commits 'moved' on top of main! git log --oneline --graph ```
steps.self_check: History is linear, no merge commits. Feature commits appear after the last main commit

steps.name: Interactive Rebase
steps.description: Use `git rebase -i HEAD~N` to clean up the last N commits: `pick` (keep), `reword` (rename), `squash` (combine into previous). Produces a clean, professional history.
steps.actions:
```bash # Create 3 'messy' commits git checkout -b feature-contact echo "

Email

" >> index.html git add index.html git commit -m "add email" echo "

Phone

" >> index.html git add index.html git commit -m "add phone oops typo" echo "

Address

" >> index.html git add index.html git commit -m "add address" # Edit the last 3 commits git rebase -i HEAD~3 ``` Editor opens: ``` pick abc123 add email pick def456 add phone oops typo pick ghi789 add address ``` Change to: ``` pick abc123 add email reword def456 add phone oops typo # Rename it squash ghi789 add address # Combine with previous ``` Save → Edit messages → Done! ```bash git log --oneline # Now 2 clean commits instead of 3! ```
steps.self_check: Commits combined and renamed. Clean history with fewer, well-named commits

steps.name: Cherry-pick — Selective Transfer
steps.description:
**Scenario:** A feature branch has a critical bugfix, but the whole branch isn't ready yet.

`git cherry-pick <sha>` copies just that one commit to the current branch — perfect for urgent fixes.
steps.actions:
**Scenario:** The feature branch has a bugfix you need, but the whole branch isn't ready.

```bash
# Create feature with a bugfix
git checkout -b feature-experimental
echo "<!-- Bugfix -->" >> index.html
git add index.html
git commit -m "Fix critical bug"

echo "<!-- Experimental -->" >> index.html
git add index.html
git commit -m "Experimental feature"

# Copy the bugfix SHA
git log --oneline
# abc1234 Experimental feature
# def5678 Fix critical bug  <- Need this one!

# Switch to main
git checkout main

# Cherry-pick only the bugfix
git cherry-pick def5678

# Bugfix is in main without the experimental feature!
git log --oneline
```
steps.self_check: Commit copied to target branch. Original commit remains in the source branch

steps.name: Git Hooks — Automation
steps.description: Create `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (make it executable) to run checks before every commit: block TODOs, validate HTML. The commit is blocked if the hook exits with code 1.
steps.actions:
**Create a pre-commit hook:**

```bash
cd ~/projects/my-portfolio

# Create the hook script
cat > .git/hooks/pre-commit << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash

echo "🔍 Running pre-commit checks..."

# Check for TODO in code
if git diff --cached | grep -i 'TODO'; then
  echo "❌ Found TODO in code! Fix before commit."
  exit 1
fi

# Check HTML
if [ -f index.html ]; then
  if ! grep -q "<!DOCTYPE html>" index.html; then
    echo "❌ Invalid HTML!"
    exit 1
  fi
fi

echo "✅ All checks passed!"
exit 0
EOF

# Make it executable
chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Test it
echo "<!-- TODO: fix this -->" >> index.html
git add index.html
git commit -m "Test"
# Commit blocked! Hook worked!
```
steps.self_check: Hook runs before every commit. Blocks commits that fail the checks

steps.name: Husky — Shared Hooks
steps.description:
Problem: `.git/hooks/` isn't committed to the repo.
Solution: Husky!

`npm install husky --save-dev` + `npx husky init` creates `.husky/` which IS committed. Everyone on the team gets the same hooks after cloning.
steps.actions:
**Problem:** Hooks in .git/hooks/ aren't committed.
**Solution:** Husky!

```bash
# Initialize npm project
npm init -y

# Install Husky
npm install husky --save-dev

# Initialize
npx husky init

# Create pre-commit hook
cat > .husky/pre-commit << 'EOF'
#!/bin/sh
echo "🔍 Checking code..."

# Check files
if git diff --cached --name-only | grep -q ".html$"; then
  echo "✅ HTML files detected"
fi

echo "✅ Pre-commit checks passed!"
EOF

chmod +x .husky/pre-commit

# Commit
git add package.json .husky/
git commit -m "Add Husky hooks"

# Now the hook works for everyone who clones!
```
steps.self_check: `.husky/` folder in repo. Hooks work for everyone who clones the project

steps.name: Git Bisect — Find the Bug
steps.description:
**Scenario:** Something worked a week ago, now it's broken. Which commit caused it?

`git bisect start` → `git bisect bad` (now) → `git bisect good <old-sha>`. Git does binary search. You answer `good` or `bad` at each step until it identifies the culprit.
steps.actions:
**Scenario:** A week ago it worked, now there's a bug. Where did it break?

```bash
# Create history with a bug
for i in {1..10}; do
  echo "Version $i" >> index.html
  git add index.html
  git commit -m "Update $i"
done

# On version 6 add a bug
git checkout HEAD~4
echo "<script>alert('bug')</script>" >> index.html
git add index.html
git commit --amend --no-edit

# Back to main
git checkout main

# Start the search
git bisect start
git bisect bad  # Current is bad
git bisect good HEAD~10  # 10 commits ago was good

# Git checks the middle commit
# Check the code, tell if good or bad:
git bisect good  # or bad

# Git narrows down until it finds the culprit!
# Finish:
git bisect reset
```
steps.self_check: Git identifies the exact commit that introduced the bug

steps.name: Reflog — Time Machine
steps.description:
**Scenario:** You accidentally deleted a branch with important work!

`git reflog` shows every operation Git ever did. Find the commit SHA, then `git checkout -b recovered <sha>` to restore it. Git remembers everything for ~90 days.
steps.actions:
**Scenario:** Accidentally deleted a branch with important commits!

```bash
# Create branch with work
git checkout -b important-work
echo "Important data" > important.txt
git add important.txt
git commit -m "Important work"

# Copy the commit SHA
git log --oneline

# Accidentally delete the branch!
git checkout main
git branch -D important-work
# Branch deleted! Commit lost!

# BUT Git remembers everything!
git reflog
# Find the line: abc1234 HEAD@{1}: commit: Important work

# Restore the commit
git checkout abc1234
# Or create a new branch:
git checkout -b recovered-work abc1234

# Data is saved!
```
steps.self_check: reflog shows all past operations. 'Deleted' commits can be recovered

steps.name: GPG Commit Signing
steps.description:
Sign commits with a GPG key to get the '✅ Verified' badge on GitHub — proving commits really came from you (not someone who stole your credentials).

Generate key → add public key to GitHub → `git config commit.gpgsign true`.
steps.actions:
**Why:** Get the verified ✅ badge on GitHub.

```bash
# Generate GPG key
gpg --full-generate-key
# Choose: RSA, 4096, no expiration, enter name and email

# View keys
gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format LONG
# sec   rsa4096/ABCD1234 ...

# Export public key
gpg --armor --export ABCD1234

# Copy all output (BEGIN - END)
# Add to GitHub: Settings → SSH and GPG keys → New GPG key

# Configure Git
git config --global user.signingkey ABCD1234
git config --global commit.gpgsign true

# Now all commits are signed!
git commit -m "Verified commit"

# On GitHub you'll see 'Verified' badge ✅
```
steps.self_check: Commits on GitHub display the green 'Verified' badge

ka.name: Git და GitHub: პრაქტიკული კურსი
ka.short_description: შექმენი რეალური პროექტი პირველი commit-დან ავტომატურ deploy-მდე
ka.description:
## 🚀 რატომ ეს კურსი?

მიდიხარ გასაუბრებაზე. რეკრუტერი ეკითხება: *"აჩვენე შენი GitHub"*. და შენ გაქვს... სიცარიელე.

**დამსაქმებლების 93%** GitHub-ს ამოწმებს გასაუბრებამდე. აქტიური პროფილის გარეშე შანსები offer-ის მისაღებად 3-ჯერ ეცემა.

## 💡 რას მიიღებ

**რეალური პორტფოლიო** რომელიც მუშაობს:
- 🌐 Live საიტი GitHub Pages-ზე
- 🔥 Commit-ების ისტორია
- ✅ ავტომატური ტესტები და CI/CD
- 🎯 Pull Request-ები და Code Review

## 📚 პროგრამა

5 პრაქტიკული გაკვეთილი:

1️⃣ **Git-ის საფუძვლები** — პირველი repo, commits, deploy
2️⃣ **ვეტები და merge** — feature ვეტები, კონფლიქტები
3️⃣ **გუნდური მუშაობა** — Fork, PR, Code Review
4️⃣ **ავტომატიზაცია** — GitHub Actions, CI/CD
5️⃣ **Pro დონე** — Rebase, cherry-pick, hooks

**მხოლოდ პრაქტიკა!** ყოველი ნაბიჯი = რეალური მოქმედება.

lessons.name: დღე 1: შენი პირველი GitHub პროექტი
lessons.short_description: 📱 ალექსმა გადაწყვიტა გახდეს დეველოპერი. პირველი ნაბიჯი — შექმენი პორტფოლიო

steps.name: გმირთან გაცნობა
steps.description:
👋 გაიცანი ალექსი — ის სწავლობს გახდეს დეველოპერი. დღეს ერთად შევქმნით შენს პირველ პორტფოლიოს GitHub-ზე!

**🎯 მიზანი:** შექმენი პირველი GitHub პროექტი

**📚 ისწავლი:**
- რა არის Git და რატომ გჭირდება
- GitHub-ის ანგარიშის შექმნა
- პირველი საიტის გამოქვეყნება ინტერნეტში

**⏱️ დრო:** 30-40 წუთი
